events.text: Time to start the app in production mode from the laptop. The deploy command calls the same up function we used during development, but it runs detached. Close your laptop and the app keeps going.
events.afterText: App running in production. Same command, same result, on the real server.
events.prose: Start the app in production mode from the laptop. The deploy command calls the same up function used during development, but runs detached so the app continues after the laptop disconnects.

events.text: Now enable autostart so the app survives a server reboot. From here on, if the server restarts, this repo comes back automatically.
events.afterText: Autostart on. The app survives reboots.
events.prose: Enable autostart so the repo survives server reboots. Once enabled, the repo is brought back automatically whenever the server restarts.

events.text: Let us list which repositories have autostart enabled. Confirms our app is in the boot list.
events.afterText: There it is, in the boot list.
events.prose: List the repositories with autostart enabled to confirm the app is in the boot list.

events.text: And to stop the app, one command from the laptop. One command up, one command down. All from your terminal.
events.afterText: Stopped. One command up, one command down.
events.prose: Stop the app with a single command from the laptop. One command brings it up, one command takes it down, all driven from the terminal.

narrations.text: Your app runs while you're connected. Production means it runs when you're gone. Three short steps, all from your laptop.

Here's the difference. In development you start the app from inside the repo over SSH. Close the session, and the app dies with it. In production you launch it detached from your laptop: the server owns the process, your connection doesn't.
And that's production mode in a nutshell. See you in the next one!
